A wild animal (or deer) dreadfully howling aloud repeatedly,
indicates the destruction of the village. The same facing the Sun,

286 Brhat SamhitTl
standing to the south of an army and crying aloud, foretells the
annihilation of the army.

If at a juncture (dawn or twilight) a flock of deer (or animals)
or the wind be to the left of an army (and be facing the Sun), a
war would break out; if it be to the right of the army, cries plea-
